{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位}}, the highest shrine rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamigoryō Shrine-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:ppp]]

{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位}}, the highest shrine rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamigoryō Shrine-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位}}, the highest shrine rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamigoryō Shrine-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]]

{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位}}, the highest shrine rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamigoryō Shrine-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamigoryō Shrine-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank}}, the highest shrine rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kamigoryō Shrine-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank}}, the highest shrine rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank}}, the highest shrine rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank}}, the highest shrine rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank}}, the highest shrine rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank}}, the highest shrine rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank}}, the highest shrine rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]]

{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank}}, the highest shrine rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]]
