It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:New]]

{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank}}, the highest shrine rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:New]]

It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank}}, the highest shrine rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇|12=draft}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=draft}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=draft}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=draft}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|12=draft}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|12=draft}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=draft}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|12=draft}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|12=draft}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|12=draft}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|12=draft}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=draft}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|12=draft}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)|12=draft}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=draft}}, the highest shrine rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=draft}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長|12=draft}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就|12=draft}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=draft}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|12=draft}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=draft}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=draft}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=draft}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=draft}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}}

{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=draft|12=draft}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|12=draft|12=draft}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|12=draft|12=draft}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=draft|12=draft}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|12=draft|12=draft}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=draft|12=draft}}, the highest shrine rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長|12=draft|12=draft}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就|12=draft|12=draft}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=draft|12=draft}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|12=draft|12=draft}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=draft|12=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft|12=draft}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft|12=draft}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=draft|12=draft}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}}

This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=draft|12=draft}}, the highest shrine rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長|12=draft|12=draft}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就|12=draft|12=draft}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=draft|12=draft}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|12=draft|12=draft}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=draft|12=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft|12=draft}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft|12=draft}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=draft|12=draft}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}}

{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=draft|12=draft}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft|12=draft}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft|12=draft}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|12=draft|12=draft}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=draft|12=draft}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|12=draft|12=draft}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|12=draft|12=draft}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=draft|12=draft}}, the highest shrine rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長|12=draft|12=draft}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就|12=draft|12=draft}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=draft|12=draft}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|12=draft|12=draft}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=draft|12=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft|12=draft}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft|12=draft}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=draft|12=draft}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=draft|12=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft|12=draft}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft|12=draft}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=draft|12=draft}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=draft|12=draft}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft|12=draft}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft|12=draft}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|12=draft|12=draft}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=draft|12=draft}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|12=draft|12=draft}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|12=draft|12=draft}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=draft|12=draft}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|12=draft|12=draft}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=draft|12=draft}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長|12=draft|12=draft}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就|12=draft|12=draft}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=draft|12=draft}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|12=draft|12=draft}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=draft|12=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=draft|12=draft}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft|12=draft}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft|12=draft}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=draft|12=draft}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}}

In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長|12=draft|12=draft}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就|12=draft|12=draft}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=draft|12=draft}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|12=draft|12=draft}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=draft|12=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft|12=draft}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft|12=draft}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=draft|12=draft}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}}

{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇|12=draft}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=draft}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=draft}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=draft}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|12=draft}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|12=draft}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=draft}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|12=draft}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|12=draft}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|12=draft}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|12=draft}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=draft}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|12=draft}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)|12=draft}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=draft}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=draft}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長|12=draft}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就|12=draft}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=draft}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|12=draft}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=draft}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=draft}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=draft}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=draft}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}}

{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)

By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank}}, the highest shrine Rank. revision.comment: 83 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:上御霊神社 up to rev 101246207
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 上御霊神社 |画像 = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = [[京都市]][[上京区]][[上御霊前通]][[烏丸通|烏丸]]東入上御霊竪町495 |ISO = JP-26 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 02|緯度秒 = 11 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 45|経度秒 = 42 |祭神 = {{Ublist| [[早良親王|崇道天皇]] | [[他戸親王]] | [[井上内親王|井上大皇后]] | [[藤原吉子|藤原大夫人]] | [[橘逸勢|橘大夫]] | [[文室宮田麻呂|文大夫]] | [[荒魂|火雷神]] | [[吉備真備|吉備大臣]] }} |創建 = [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]5年（[[863年]]） |社格 = 旧[[府社]] |札所等 = [[神仏霊場巡拝の道]]第100番（京都第20番）
[[京洛八社集印めぐり]] |例祭 = [[5月18日]]（御霊祭） |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |}} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''上御霊神社'''（かみごりょうじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[上京区]]上御霊竪町にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[府社]]。上御霊神社という社名は[[下御霊神社]]に対応するもので、現在は宗教法人としての正式名を「御靈神社」としている。 == 歴史 == そもそもは[[桓武天皇]]の時代、各地で疫病が流行していた。これは[[御霊信仰|御霊]]の[[祟り]]であるとして、[[延暦]]13年（[[794年]]）[[5月]]、[[早良親王]]（崇道天皇）の御霊をこの地に祀ったのが始めだとされる。また、この地には[[平安京|平安]]遷都以前から出雲氏の[[氏寺]]・上出雲寺があり、当社の元はその[[鎮守社]]であったともされる{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=京都・山城寺院神社大事典 |publisher=株式会社　平凡社 |year=1997-12-19- |page=166-167 |isbn=4-582-13401-7}}。 [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]5年（[[863年]]）[[5月20日 (旧暦)|5月20日]]、[[平安京]]の[[神泉苑]]で[[御霊会]]が催された。この時に慰霊された御霊は崇道天皇（[[早良親王]]）・[[伊予親王]]・藤原夫人（[[藤原吉子]]）・観察使（[[藤原仲成]]）・[[橘逸勢]]・[[文室宮田麻呂|文屋宮田麿]]らの六所御霊であった。この御霊会が当社および下御霊神社の創祀であるとしている。 後年には祭神のうち伊予親王と観察使（藤原仲成）が[[井上内親王|井上大皇后]]と[[他戸親王]]に変わり、[[荒魂|火雷神]]と[[吉備真備|吉備大臣]]（吉備聖霊）が追加されている。 [[平安時代]]には現在の[[下御霊神社]]を下出雲寺御霊堂、当社は上出雲寺御霊堂と称した。朝廷の篤い崇敬を受け、[[至徳 (日本)|至徳]]元年（[[1384年]]）には[[正一位]]の[[神階]]を授けられている。 [[室町時代]]の[[文正]]2年（[[1467年]]）[[1月18日 (旧暦)|1月18日]]には、失脚した[[管領]]の[[畠山政長]]が当社境内の森に布陣し、そこに[[畠山義就]]が攻め寄せて[[御霊合戦|上御霊神社の戦い]]が行われた。この戦いは[[応仁の乱]]の前哨戦となったことから当地は「応仁の乱発祥の地」とされる。 なお、[[延喜式神名帳]]に記載される出雲井於神社（式内大社）や出雲高野神社（式内小社）を当社に比定する説もあるが、継続性は不詳であり、神社側の由諸ではこれらの式内社について言及していない。 [[明治]]時代になると[[府社]]に列せられている。 == 祭神 == 現在の主祭神（本殿八座）は以下の八柱で、「八所御霊」と称される。 * 崇道天皇（[[早良親王]]。[[光仁天皇]]の皇子） * [[井上内親王|井上大皇后]]（光仁天皇の[[皇后]]） * [[他戸親王]]（光仁天皇の皇子） * 藤原大夫人（[[藤原吉子]]、[[桓武天皇]]皇子[[伊予親王]]の母） * 橘大夫（[[橘逸勢]]） * 文大夫（[[文室宮田麻呂]]） * 火雷神（以上六柱の[[荒魂]]） * 吉備大臣（[[吉備真備]]） また、相殿（相殿五座）としては以下の五柱が祀られている。 * 三社明神（[[小倉実起]]、[[小倉公連]]、[[中納言]]典待局（小倉実起の娘）、[[竹淵季伴|小倉季判]]） * 和光明神（[[菅原和子]]・若宮） 祭神は9世紀前半から民衆の間で広まり、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]5年（[[863年]]）には公式の[[御霊会]]で祭られるようになった御霊信仰が元になっている。当初の御霊会で祭られたのは崇道天皇、伊予親王、藤原夫人、[[観察使]]（[[藤原仲成]]{{Efn|[[藤原広嗣]]を当てる説もあるが、広嗣の時代には観察使が設置されていない}}）、橘大夫（橘逸勢）、文大夫（文室宮田麻呂）の六所御霊であった。後年追加された火雷神と吉備大臣（吉備聖霊）の二神について、火雷神は[[菅原道真]]、吉備聖霊は[[吉備内親王]]、または伝承にある井上内親王が産んだ皇子とする説、さらに火雷神は落雷を司る雷精で、吉備聖霊は鬼魅（災事を起こさせる霊力）であると解釈する説もある[[森田悌]]「王朝政治」（[[講談社学術文庫]]） 187-193p。 相殿に小倉実起・小倉公連・中納言典待局{{Efn|小倉実起の娘で[[霊元天皇]]に仕える。[[小倉事件]]を参照のこと。}}・小倉（竹淵）季判、若宮に和光明神（菅原和子）を配祀する。 これらの諸神は（吉備真備を除いて）いずれも政争に巻き込まれて憤死した人々で、その[[怨霊]]を慰めるために創建されたのが当社である。 == 境内 == * 本殿 * 拝所 * [[神明神社]] - 祭神：[[天照大御神]] * [[稲荷神社]] - 祭神：[[稲荷神]] * [[厳島神社]] - 祭神：[[宗像三女神]] * 花御所[[八幡宮]] - 祭神：[[八幡神]]。[[足利義満]]の[[花の御所]]の[[鎮守社]]であったとされる。 * 大舞神社 * [[天満宮]]社 - 祭神：[[菅原道真]] * [[多度大社|多度神社]] - 祭神：[[アマツヒコネ|天津彦根命]] * [[貴船神社]] - 祭神：[[淤加美神|高龗神]] * [[粟島神社]] - 祭神：[[淡島神]] * [[白髪神社]] * 長宮三十社 - 春原社、[[荒神|荒神社]]、稲葉神社、[[今宮神社 (京都市)|今宮神社]]、[[熊野神社]]、[[愛宕神社]]、[[熱田神社]]、[[多賀神社]]、[[厳島神社|厳嶋神社]]、[[猿田彦神社]]、[[貴船神社|貴布禰社]]、丹生神社、[[梅宮神社]]、[[八坂神社]]、[[廣田神社]]、[[吉田神社]]、[[日吉神社]]、[[住吉神社]]、[[龍田神社]]、[[広瀬神社|廣瀬神社]]、[[大和神社]]、[[石上神宮|石上神社]]、[[大神神社|大神社]]、大原神社、[[平野神社]]、[[春日神社]]、[[松尾神社]]、[[八幡神社]]、[[賀茂神社]]、[[鴨神社]] * 神変塚 - 神変大菩薩（[[役行者]]）の塚。 * 神楽殿 * 社務所 * 絵馬所 - [[宝暦]]年間（[[1751年]] - [[1764年]]）に寄進された内裏賢所権殿を改築したもの。 * 御車舎 * 応仁の乱 発端 御霊合戦旧跡 碑 - [[応仁の乱]]のもとになった[[御霊合戦]]があった、と記されている。 [[ファイル:Kamigoryo Ohnin War.jpg|サムネイル|応仁の乱 発端 御霊合戦旧跡 碑]] * 松尾芭蕉句碑 - [[松尾芭蕉]]が[[元禄]]3年（[[1690年]]）12月に参詣し俳句を奉納。「半日は神を友にや年忘れ」。 * 新村出の歌碑 - [[広辞苑]]編者で有名な[[新村出]]の歌が碑に刻まれている。新村出は上御霊神社氏子小山中溝町に住み、八十歳の誕生日に上御霊神社に参拝の折、献詠したもの。「上御霊のみやしろに詣でてよめる 千早振（ちはやぶる）神のみめぐみ深くして 八十（やそ）ぢに満つる 幸を得にけり」 * 四脚門 - 南門。[[伏見城]]の四脚門を移築したもの。 * 楼門 - 西門。[[寛政]]年間（[[1789年]] - [[1801年]]）に再建。 Gate of Kamigoryo Shrine in Kyoto 01.jpg|代替文=四脚門（南門）|四脚門（南門） Kamigoryo Niimura.jpg|新村出の歌碑 鳥居と楼門（西門）.jpg|代替文=鳥居と楼門（西門）|鳥居と楼門（西門） 御車社.jpg|代替文=御車社|御車社 Kamigoryo Shrine in Kyoto Two-storied gate 01.jpg|代替文=楼門（西門）|楼門（西門） == 祭事 == {| class="wikitable" |+御霊神社祭日 |- |[[歳旦祭]] |一月一日 |- |[[節分祭]] |節分の日 |- |[[紀元祭]] |二月十一日 |- |[[祈年祭]] |春分の日 |- |御霊祭社頭之儀 |五月一日 |- |御霊祭渡御之儀 |五月十八日 |- |御内儀春季祭 |五月二十日 |- |[[大祓式]] |六月三十日 |- |例祭 |八月十八日 |- |[[七五三]]祝 |十一月十五日 |- |火焚祭 |十一月十八日 |- |[[新嘗祭]] |十一月二十三日 |- |御内儀秋季祭 |十一月二十八日 |- |大祓式 |十二月三十一日 |- |月次祭 |毎月一日 毎月十八日 |} {| class="wikitable" |+境内社 |- |福壽稲荷社祭 |二月初午日 |- |御霊天満宮祭 |三月二十五日 |- |花御所八幡宮祭 |九月十五日 |- |神明社祭 |十月十七日 |} 例祭の御霊祭は[[明治]]までは[[8月18日 (旧暦)|8月18日]]（旧暦）に行われていたが、現在では5月18日（新暦）に行われている。御霊祭は京都（洛中）で最も古い祭りと言われる平竹南部 謹書。 === 囀市（さえずりいち） === 5月を除く毎月18日の朝から、境内で小規模な[[市場|市]]が開催されている。 == 前後の札所 == ; [[神仏霊場巡拝の道]] : 99 [[相国寺]]　-　'''100 御霊神社（上御霊神社）'''　-　101 [[賀茂御祖神社]] == 交通 == * 最寄駅 : [[京都市営地下鉄]][[京都市営地下鉄烏丸線|烏丸線]][[鞍馬口駅]]下車、徒歩3分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[御霊信仰]] * [[下御霊神社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html 上御霊神社]（京都府神社庁） * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=京都府}} {{洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かみこりようしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:上京区の歴史]] [[Category:上京区の建築物]] [[Category:通称]] [[Category:洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝]] [[Category:京都府の景観重要建造物]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[上御霊神社]] to [[Kamigoryō Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 上御霊神社 |画像 = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = [[京都市]][[上京区]][[上御霊前通]][[烏丸通|烏丸]]東入上御霊竪町495 |ISO = JP-26 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 02|緯度秒 = 11 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 45|経度秒 = 42 |祭神 = {{Ublist| [[早良親王|崇道天皇]] | [[他戸親王]] | [[井上内親王|井上大皇后]] | [[藤原吉子|藤原大夫人]] | [[橘逸勢|橘大夫]] | [[文室宮田麻呂|文大夫]] | [[荒魂|火雷神]] | [[吉備真備|吉備大臣]] }} |創建 = [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]5年（[[863年]]） |社格 = 旧[[府社]] |札所等 = [[神仏霊場巡拝の道]]第100番（京都第20番）
[[京洛八社集印めぐり]] |例祭 = [[5月18日]]（御霊祭） |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |}} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''上御霊神社'''（かみごりょうじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[上京区]]上御霊竪町にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[府社]]。上御霊神社という社名は[[下御霊神社]]に対応するもので、現在は宗教法人としての正式名を「御靈神社」としている。 == 歴史 == そもそもは[[桓武天皇]]の時代、各地で疫病が流行していた。これは[[御霊信仰|御霊]]の[[祟り]]であるとして、[[延暦]]13年（[[794年]]）[[5月]]、[[早良親王]]（崇道天皇）の御霊をこの地に祀ったのが始めだとされる。また、この地には[[平安京|平安]]遷都以前から出雲氏の[[氏寺]]・上出雲寺があり、当社の元はその[[鎮守社]]であったともされる{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=京都・山城寺院神社大事典 |publisher=株式会社　平凡社 |year=1997-12-19- |page=166-167 |isbn=4-582-13401-7}}。 [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]5年（[[863年]]）[[5月20日 (旧暦)|5月20日]]、[[平安京]]の[[神泉苑]]で[[御霊会]]が催された。この時に慰霊された御霊は崇道天皇（[[早良親王]]）・[[伊予親王]]・藤原夫人（[[藤原吉子]]）・観察使（[[藤原仲成]]）・[[橘逸勢]]・[[文室宮田麻呂|文屋宮田麿]]らの六所御霊であった。この御霊会が当社および下御霊神社の創祀であるとしている。 後年には祭神のうち伊予親王と観察使（藤原仲成）が[[井上内親王|井上大皇后]]と[[他戸親王]]に変わり、[[荒魂|火雷神]]と[[吉備真備|吉備大臣]]（吉備聖霊）が追加されている。 [[平安時代]]には現在の[[下御霊神社]]を下出雲寺御霊堂、当社は上出雲寺御霊堂と称した。朝廷の篤い崇敬を受け、[[至徳 (日本)|至徳]]元年（[[1384年]]）には[[正一位]]の[[神階]]を授けられている。 [[室町時代]]の[[文正]]2年（[[1467年]]）[[1月18日 (旧暦)|1月18日]]には、失脚した[[管領]]の[[畠山政長]]が当社境内の森に布陣し、そこに[[畠山義就]]が攻め寄せて[[御霊合戦|上御霊神社の戦い]]が行われた。この戦いは[[応仁の乱]]の前哨戦となったことから当地は「応仁の乱発祥の地」とされる。 なお、[[延喜式神名帳]]に記載される出雲井於神社（式内大社）や出雲高野神社（式内小社）を当社に比定する説もあるが、継続性は不詳であり、神社側の由諸ではこれらの式内社について言及していない。 [[明治]]時代になると[[府社]]に列せられている。 == 祭神 == 現在の主祭神（本殿八座）は以下の八柱で、「八所御霊」と称される。 * 崇道天皇（[[早良親王]]。[[光仁天皇]]の皇子） * [[井上内親王|井上大皇后]]（光仁天皇の[[皇后]]） * [[他戸親王]]（光仁天皇の皇子） * 藤原大夫人（[[藤原吉子]]、[[桓武天皇]]皇子[[伊予親王]]の母） * 橘大夫（[[橘逸勢]]） * 文大夫（[[文室宮田麻呂]]） * 火雷神（以上六柱の[[荒魂]]） * 吉備大臣（[[吉備真備]]） また、相殿（相殿五座）としては以下の五柱が祀られている。 * 三社明神（[[小倉実起]]、[[小倉公連]]、[[中納言]]典待局（小倉実起の娘）、[[竹淵季伴|小倉季判]]） * 和光明神（[[菅原和子]]・若宮） 祭神は9世紀前半から民衆の間で広まり、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]5年（[[863年]]）には公式の[[御霊会]]で祭られるようになった御霊信仰が元になっている。当初の御霊会で祭られたのは崇道天皇、伊予親王、藤原夫人、[[観察使]]（[[藤原仲成]]{{Efn|[[藤原広嗣]]を当てる説もあるが、広嗣の時代には観察使が設置されていない}}）、橘大夫（橘逸勢）、文大夫（文室宮田麻呂）の六所御霊であった。後年追加された火雷神と吉備大臣（吉備聖霊）の二神について、火雷神は[[菅原道真]]、吉備聖霊は[[吉備内親王]]、または伝承にある井上内親王が産んだ皇子とする説、さらに火雷神は落雷を司る雷精で、吉備聖霊は鬼魅（災事を起こさせる霊力）であると解釈する説もある[[森田悌]]「王朝政治」（[[講談社学術文庫]]） 187-193p。 相殿に小倉実起・小倉公連・中納言典待局{{Efn|小倉実起の娘で[[霊元天皇]]に仕える。[[小倉事件]]を参照のこと。}}・小倉（竹淵）季判、若宮に和光明神（菅原和子）を配祀する。 これらの諸神は（吉備真備を除いて）いずれも政争に巻き込まれて憤死した人々で、その[[怨霊]]を慰めるために創建されたのが当社である。 == 境内 == * 本殿 * 拝所 * [[神明神社]] - 祭神：[[天照大御神]] * [[稲荷神社]] - 祭神：[[稲荷神]] * [[厳島神社]] - 祭神：[[宗像三女神]] * 花御所[[八幡宮]] - 祭神：[[八幡神]]。[[足利義満]]の[[花の御所]]の[[鎮守社]]であったとされる。 * 大舞神社 * [[天満宮]]社 - 祭神：[[菅原道真]] * [[多度大社|多度神社]] - 祭神：[[アマツヒコネ|天津彦根命]] * [[貴船神社]] - 祭神：[[淤加美神|高龗神]] * [[粟島神社]] - 祭神：[[淡島神]] * [[白髪神社]] * 長宮三十社 - 春原社、[[荒神|荒神社]]、稲葉神社、[[今宮神社 (京都市)|今宮神社]]、[[熊野神社]]、[[愛宕神社]]、[[熱田神社]]、[[多賀神社]]、[[厳島神社|厳嶋神社]]、[[猿田彦神社]]、[[貴船神社|貴布禰社]]、丹生神社、[[梅宮神社]]、[[八坂神社]]、[[廣田神社]]、[[吉田神社]]、[[日吉神社]]、[[住吉神社]]、[[龍田神社]]、[[広瀬神社|廣瀬神社]]、[[大和神社]]、[[石上神宮|石上神社]]、[[大神神社|大神社]]、大原神社、[[平野神社]]、[[春日神社]]、[[松尾神社]]、[[八幡神社]]、[[賀茂神社]]、[[鴨神社]] |[[大祓式]] |六月三十日 |- |例祭 |八月十八日 |- |[[七五三]]祝 |十一月十五日 |- |火焚祭 |十一月十八日 |- |[[新嘗祭]] |十一月二十三日 |- |御内儀秋季祭 |十一月二十八日 |- |大祓式 |十二月三十一日 |- |月次祭 |毎月一日 毎月十八日 |} {| class="wikitable" |+境内社 |- |福壽稲荷社祭 |二月初午日 |- |御霊天満宮祭 |三月二十五日 |- |花御所八幡宮祭 |九月十五日 |- |神明社祭 |十月十七日 |} 例祭の御霊祭は[[明治]]までは[[8月18日 (旧暦)|8月18日]]（旧暦）に行われていたが、現在では5月18日（新暦）に行われている。御霊祭は京都（洛中）で最も古い祭りと言われる平竹南部 謹書。 === 囀市（さえずりいち） === 5月を除く毎月18日の朝から、境内で小規模な[[市場|市]]が開催されている。 == 前後の札所 == ; [[神仏霊場巡拝の道]] : 99 [[相国寺]]　-　'''100 御霊神社（上御霊神社）'''　-　101 [[賀茂御祖神社]] == 交通 == * 最寄駅 : [[京都市営地下鉄]][[京都市営地下鉄烏丸線|烏丸線]][[鞍馬口駅]]下車、徒歩3分 == 脚注 == {{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=draft|12=draft}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft|12=draft}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft|12=draft}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|12=draft|12=draft}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=draft|12=draft}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|12=draft|12=draft}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|12=draft|12=draft}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=draft|12=draft}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|12=draft|12=draft}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=draft|12=draft}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長|12=draft|12=draft}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就|12=draft|12=draft}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=draft|12=draft}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|12=draft|12=draft}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=draft|12=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft|12=draft}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=draft|12=draft}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=draft|12=draft}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamigoryo Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]]

By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]]
