These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]]

{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|qq=}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimogoryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimogoryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kanmu|qq=}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/goryō spirits|qq=}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku|qq=}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sawara|qq=}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian shrine|qq=}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|qq=}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen-en|qq=}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimogoryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Makibi|qq=}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoku|qq=}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|qq=}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatakeyama Masanaga|qq=}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatakeyama Yoshinari|qq=}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnin War|qq=}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|qq=}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sawara|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōnin|qq=}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Hayanari|qq=}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Makibi|qq=}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|qq=}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|qq=}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/goryō beliefs|qq=}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]]

In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoku|qq=}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|qq=}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatakeyama Masanaga|qq=}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatakeyama Yoshinari|qq=}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnin War|qq=}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|qq=}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sawara|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōnin|qq=}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Hayanari|qq=}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Makibi|qq=}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|qq=}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|qq=}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/goryō beliefs|qq=}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamigyō Ward|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō Ward|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō|qq=}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Hayanari|qq=}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Makibi|qq=}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|qq=}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kanmu|qq=}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/goryō spirits|qq=}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku|qq=}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sawara|qq=}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian shrine|qq=}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|qq=}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen-en|qq=}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Makibi|qq=}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoku|qq=}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|qq=}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatakeyama Masanaga|qq=}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatakeyama Yoshinari|qq=}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnin War|qq=}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|qq=}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sawara|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōnin|qq=}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Hayanari|qq=}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Makibi|qq=}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|qq=}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|qq=}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/goryō beliefs|qq=}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]]

Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Makibi|qq=}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoku|qq=}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|qq=}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatakeyama Masanaga|qq=}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatakeyama Yoshinari|qq=}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnin War|qq=}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|qq=}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sawara|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|qq=}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/goryō beliefs|qq=}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. {{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|qq=}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|ja|上京区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kanmu|qq=}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|goryō spirits|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/goryō spirits|qq=}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku|qq=}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sawara|qq=}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|guardian shrine|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian shrine|qq=}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|qq=}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen-en|qq=}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Makibi|qq=}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|qq=}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatakeyama Masanaga|qq=}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatakeyama Yoshinari|qq=}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnin War|qq=}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|qq=}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen-en|qq=}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Makibi|qq=}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|ja|至徳 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoku|qq=}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|qq=}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|ja|畠山政長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatakeyama Masanaga|qq=}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|ja|畠山義就|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatakeyama Yoshinari|qq=}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnin War|qq=}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|qq=}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sawara|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōnin|qq=}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|ja|橘逸勢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Hayanari|qq=}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Makibi|qq=}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|qq=}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|qq=}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|goryō beliefs|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/goryō beliefs|qq=}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō Ward]], Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō|qq=}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=}}|[[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]]|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}}|[[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]]}} |consecration_year = [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE) |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|qq=}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of [[:en:Goryō|goryō spirits]], and in [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|qq=}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=}} ritual was held at the [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]] to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}} and [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], were added to the original six spirits. In the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By [[:en:Shitoku|Shitoku]] 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|qq=}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when [[:en:Hatakeyama Masanaga|Hatakeyama Masanaga]] clashed with [[:en:Hatakeyama Yoshinari|Hatakeyama Yoshinari]], an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|qq=}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]]) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * [[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]] * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|qq=}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|qq=}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]] in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]]

The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|qq=}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]]) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * [[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]] * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|qq=}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|qq=}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]] in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:上京区の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō Ward]], Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō|qq=}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=}}|[[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]]|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}}|[[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]]}} |consecration_year = [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE) |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|qq=}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of [[:en:Goryō|goryō spirits]], and in [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|qq=}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=}} ritual was held at the [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]] to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}} and [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], were added to the original six spirits. In the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By [[:en:Shitoku|Shitoku]] 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|qq=}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when [[:en:Hatakeyama Masanaga|Hatakeyama Masanaga]] clashed with [[:en:Hatakeyama Yoshinari|Hatakeyama Yoshinari]], an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|qq=}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]]) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * [[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]] * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|qq=}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|qq=}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]] in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:上京区の建築物]] [[Category:上京区の歴史]]

During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]]) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * [[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]] * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|qq=}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|qq=}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]] in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:上京区の建築物]] [[Category:上京区の歴史]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō Ward]], Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō|qq=}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=}}|[[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]]|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}}|[[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]]}} |consecration_year = [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE) |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|qq=}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of [[:en:Goryō|goryō spirits]], and in [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|qq=}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=}} ritual was held at the [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]] to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}} and [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], were added to the original six spirits. In the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By [[:en:Shitoku|Shitoku]] 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|qq=}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when [[:en:Hatakeyama Masanaga|Hatakeyama Masanaga]] clashed with [[:en:Hatakeyama Yoshinari|Hatakeyama Yoshinari]], an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|qq=}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]]) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * [[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]] * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|qq=}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|qq=}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]] in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:上京区の建築物]] [[Category:上京区の歴史]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:京都府の景観重要建造物]]
