{{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|lt=Fu-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city |location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|qid=Q1321824}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.036389|N|135.761667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|lt=Fu-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of [[:en:Goryō|goryō spirits]], and in [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=}} ritual was held at the [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]] to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神}} and [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], were added to the original six spirits. In the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By [[:en:Shitoku|Shitoku]] 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when [[:en:Hatakeyama Masanaga|Hatakeyama Masanaga]] clashed with [[:en:Hatakeyama Yoshinari|Hatakeyama Yoshinari]], an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|lt=Fu-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]]) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * [[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]] * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]] in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Common names]] {{Commons category|Kamigoryō-jinja}} [[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]]

Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]]|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}}|[[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]]}} |consecration_year = [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE) |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|qq=|1=Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10444949}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city |location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|qid=Q1321824}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.036389|N|135.761667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of [[:en:Goryō|goryō spirits]], and in [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ritual was held at the [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]] to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} and [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], were added to the original six spirits. In the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By [[:en:Shitoku|Shitoku]] 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when [[:en:Hatakeyama Masanaga|Hatakeyama Masanaga]] clashed with [[:en:Hatakeyama Yoshinari|Hatakeyama Yoshinari]], an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]]) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * [[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]] * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]] in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamigoryō Shrine]] [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Common names]] {{Commons category|Kamigoryō-jinja}} [[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]]

Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 早良親王|1=Prince Sawara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079634}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}}|[[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]]|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}}|[[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]]}} |consecration_year = [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE) |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|qq=|1=Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10444949}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city |location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|qid=Q1321824}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.036389|N|135.761667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of [[:en:Goryō|goryō spirits]], and in [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ritual was held at the [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]] to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} and [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], were added to the original six spirits. In the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By [[:en:Shitoku|Shitoku]] 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when [[:en:Hatakeyama Masanaga|Hatakeyama Masanaga]] clashed with [[:en:Hatakeyama Yoshinari|Hatakeyama Yoshinari]], an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]]) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * [[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]] * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]] in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Common names]] {{Commons category|Kamigoryō-jinja}} [[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]] {{wikidata link|Q11359018}}

based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|qq=|1=Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10444949}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city |location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|qid=Q1321824}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.036389|N|135.761667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of [[:en:Goryō|goryō spirits]], and in [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ritual was held at the [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]] to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} and [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], were added to the original six spirits. In the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By [[:en:Shitoku|Shitoku]] 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when [[:en:Hatakeyama Masanaga|Hatakeyama Masanaga]] clashed with [[:en:Hatakeyama Yoshinari|Hatakeyama Yoshinari]], an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]]) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * [[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]] * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]] in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Common names]] {{Commons category|Kamigoryō-jinja}} [[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]] {{wikidata link|Q11359018}}

Pilgrimage|qq=|1=Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10444949}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city |location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|WD=Q1321824}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.036389|N|135.761667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of [[:en:Goryō|goryō spirits]], and in [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ritual was held at the [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]] to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} and [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], were added to the original six spirits. In the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By [[:en:Shitoku|Shitoku]] 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when [[:en:Hatakeyama Masanaga|Hatakeyama Masanaga]] clashed with [[:en:Hatakeyama Yoshinari|Hatakeyama Yoshinari]], an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]]) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * [[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]] * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]] in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]]) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince reflects the historical importance of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]] in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. wikidata|WD=Q10444949}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city |location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|WD=Q1321824}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.036389|N|135.761667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of [[:en:Goryō|goryō spirits]], and in [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ritual was held at the [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]] to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} and [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], were added to the original six spirits. In the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By [[:en:Shitoku|Shitoku]] 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when [[:en:Hatakeyama Masanaga|Hatakeyama Masanaga]] clashed with [[:en:Hatakeyama Yoshinari|Hatakeyama Yoshinari]], an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]]) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == By [[:en:Shitoku|Shitoku]] 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when [[:en:Hatakeyama Masanaga|Hatakeyama Masanaga]] clashed with [[:en:Hatakeyama Yoshinari|Hatakeyama Yoshinari]], an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]]) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * [[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]] * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]] in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Common names]] {{Commons category|Kamigoryō-jinja}} [[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]] {{wikidata link|Q11359018}}

03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG |location = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō Ward]], Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|1=Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10444949}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city |location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|WD=Q1321824}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.036389|N|135.761667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of [[:en:Goryō|goryō spirits]], and in [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ritual was held at the [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]] to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} and [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], were added to the original six spirits. In the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By [[:en:Shitoku|Shitoku]] 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when [[:en:Hatakeyama Masanaga|Hatakeyama Masanaga]] clashed with [[:en:Hatakeyama Yoshinari|Hatakeyama Yoshinari]], an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]]) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * [[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]] * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]] in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Common names]] {{Commons category|Kamigoryō-jinja}} [[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]] {{wikidata link|Q11359018}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By [[:en:Shitoku|Shitoku]] 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when [[:en:Hatakeyama Masanaga|Hatakeyama Masanaga]] clashed with [[:en:Hatakeyama Yoshinari|Hatakeyama Yoshinari]], an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]]) * {{ill|Empress Dowager * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]] in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. Pilgrimage|1=Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10444949}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city |location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|WD=Q1321824}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.036389|N|135.761667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of [[:en:Goryō|goryō spirits]], and in [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ritual was held at the [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]] to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} and [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], were added to the original six spirits. In the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By [[:en:Shitoku|Shitoku]] 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when [[:en:Hatakeyama Masanaga|Hatakeyama Masanaga]] clashed with [[:en:Hatakeyama Yoshinari|Hatakeyama Yoshinari]], an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} and [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], were added to the original six spirits. In the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By [[:en:Shitoku|Shitoku]] 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when [[:en:Hatakeyama Masanaga|Hatakeyama Masanaga]] clashed with [[:en:Hatakeyama Yoshinari|Hatakeyama Yoshinari]], an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], son of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]]) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * [[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]] * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]] Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]] in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Common names]] {{Commons category|Kamigoryō-jinja}} [[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]] {{wikidata link|Q11359018}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Pilgrimage|1=Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10444949}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city |location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|WD=Q1321824}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.036389|N|135.761667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of [[:en:Goryō|goryō spirits]], and in [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ritual was held at the [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]] to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} and [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], were added to the original six spirits. In the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By [[:en:Shitoku|Shitoku]] 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when [[:en:Hatakeyama Masanaga|Hatakeyama Masanaga]] clashed with [[:en:Hatakeyama Yoshinari|Hatakeyama Yoshinari]], an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear.
