Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|1=Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10444949}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city |location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|WD=Q1321824}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.036389|N|135.761667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|WD=Q3273210|lt=goryō spirits}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsenen|en|Shinsenen|WD=Q11590468|lt=Shinsen-en}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|en|Shitoku|WD=Q1146202|lt=Shitoku}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|en|Hatakeyama Masanaga|WD=Q5680891|lt=Hatakeyama Masanaga}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|en|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|WD=Q11578676|lt=Hatakeyama Yoshinari}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|en|Emperor Kōnin|WD=Q958662|lt=Emperor Kōnin}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|en|Tachibana no Hayanari|WD=Q1062686|lt=Tachibana no Hayanari}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|WD=Q3273210|lt=goryō beliefs}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:上御霊神社]] {{Commons category|Kamigoryō-jinja}} [[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]] {{wikidata link|Q11359018}} [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|en|Tachibana no Hayanari|WD=Q1062686|lt=Tachibana no Hayanari}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|WD=Q3273210|lt=goryō beliefs}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:上御霊神社]] {{Commons category|Kamigoryō-jinja}} [[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]] {{wikidata link|Q11359018}} [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

کامی‌گوریو]][[ja:上御霊神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kamigoryō-jinja]][[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]][[ja:上御霊神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG |location = {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō Ward}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 早良親王|1=Prince Sawara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079634}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|en|Tachibana no Hayanari|WD=Q1062686|lt=Tachibana no Hayanari}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|1=Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10444949}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city |location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|WD=Q1321824}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.036389|N|135.761667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|WD=Q3273210|lt=goryō spirits}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsenen|en|Shinsenen|WD=Q11590468|lt=Shinsen-en}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|en|Shitoku|WD=Q1146202|lt=Shitoku}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|en|Hatakeyama Masanaga|WD=Q5680891|lt=Hatakeyama Masanaga}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|en|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|WD=Q11578676|lt=Hatakeyama Yoshinari}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|en|Emperor Kōnin|WD=Q958662|lt=Emperor Kōnin}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|en|Tachibana no Hayanari|WD=Q1062686|lt=Tachibana no Hayanari}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|WD=Q3273210|lt=goryō beliefs}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:上御霊神社]] {{Commons category|Kamigoryō-jinja}} [[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]] {{wikidata link|Q11359018}} [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|en|Shitoku|WD=Q1146202|lt=Shitoku|1=Shitoku}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|en|Hatakeyama Masanaga|WD=Q5680891|lt=Hatakeyama Masanaga|1=Hatakeyama Masanaga}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|en|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|WD=Q11578676|lt=Hatakeyama Yoshinari|1=Hatakeyama Yoshinari}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|en|Emperor Kōnin|WD=Q958662|lt=Emperor Kōnin|1=Emperor Kōnin}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|en|Tachibana no Hayanari|WD=Q1062686|lt=Tachibana no Hayanari|1=Tachibana no Hayanari}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|WD=Q3273210|lt=goryō beliefs|1=Goryō}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:上御霊神社]] {{Commons category|Kamigoryō-jinja}} [[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]] {{wikidata link|Q11359018}} [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

of Kamigata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10444949}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city |location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|WD=Q1321824|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.036389|N|135.761667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō-ku|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|WD=Q3273210|lt=goryō spirits|1=Goryō}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine|1=Chinjusha}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsenen|en|Shinsenen|WD=Q11590468|lt=Shinsen-en|1=Shinsenen}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|en|Shitoku|WD=Q1146202|lt=Shitoku|1=Shitoku}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|en|Hatakeyama Masanaga|WD=Q5680891|lt=Hatakeyama Masanaga|1=Hatakeyama Masanaga}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|en|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|WD=Q11578676|lt=Hatakeyama Yoshinari|1=Hatakeyama Yoshinari}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}, son of {{ill|Emperor on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|1=Sacred Sites of Kamigata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10444949}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city |location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|WD=Q1321824|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.036389|N|135.761667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō-ku|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|WD=Q3273210|lt=goryō spirits|1=Goryō}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine|1=Chinjusha}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsenen|en|Shinsenen|WD=Q11590468|lt=Shinsen-en|1=Shinsenen}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|en|Shitoku|WD=Q1146202|lt=Shitoku|1=Shitoku}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|en|Hatakeyama Masanaga|WD=Q5680891|lt=Hatakeyama Masanaga|1=Hatakeyama Masanaga}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|en|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|WD=Q11578676|lt=Hatakeyama Yoshinari|1=Hatakeyama Yoshinari}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|en|Emperor Kōnin|WD=Q958662|lt=Emperor Kōnin|1=Emperor Kōnin}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|en|Tachibana no Hayanari|WD=Q1062686|lt=Tachibana no Hayanari|1=Tachibana no Hayanari}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|WD=Q3273210|lt=goryō beliefs|1=Goryō}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:上御霊神社]] {{Commons category|Kamigoryō-jinja}} [[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]] {{wikidata link|Q11359018}} [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

importance of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|WD=Q3273210|lt=goryō beliefs|1=Goryō}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:上御霊神社]] {{Commons category|Kamigoryō-jinja}} [[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]] {{wikidata link|Q11359018}} [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kamigoryō-jinja]][[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]][[ja:上御霊神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamigoryō-jinja]][[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]][[ja:上御霊神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamigoryō-jinja]][[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]][[ja:上御霊神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kamigoryō-jinja]][[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]][[ja:上御霊神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamigoryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kamigoryo Shrine140516NI2.JPG |location = {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō Ward|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}}, Kamigoryō Tatemachi 495 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 02|Latitude seconds = 11 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 45|Longitude seconds = 42 |deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 早良親王|1=Prince Sawara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079634}} (Prince Sawara)|{{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}}|{{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|{{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}}|{{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|en|Tachibana no Hayanari|WD=Q1062686|lt=Tachibana no Hayanari|1=Tachibana no Hayanari}}|{{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}}|{{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}}|{{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}}}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 5 (863 CE) |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|1=Sacred Sites of Kamigata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10444949}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city |location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|WD=Q1321824|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.036389|N|135.761667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō-ku|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|WD=Q3273210|lt=goryō spirits|1=Goryō}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine|1=Chinjusha}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsenen|en|Shinsenen|WD=Q11590468|lt=Shinsen-en|1=Shinsenen}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|en|Shitoku|WD=Q1146202|lt=Shitoku|1=Shitoku}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|en|Hatakeyama Masanaga|WD=Q5680891|lt=Hatakeyama Masanaga|1=Hatakeyama Masanaga}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|en|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|WD=Q11578676|lt=Hatakeyama Yoshinari|1=Hatakeyama Yoshinari}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|en|Emperor Kōnin|WD=Q958662|lt=Emperor Kōnin|1=Emperor Kōnin}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|en|Tachibana no Hayanari|WD=Q1062686|lt=Tachibana no Hayanari|1=Tachibana no Hayanari}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|WD=Q3273210|lt=goryō beliefs|1=Goryō}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:上御霊神社]] {{Commons category|Kamigoryō-jinja}} [[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]] {{wikidata link|Q11359018}} [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|en|Hatakeyama Masanaga|WD=Q5680891|lt=Hatakeyama Masanaga|1=Hatakeyama Masanaga}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|en|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|WD=Q11578676|lt=Hatakeyama Yoshinari|1=Hatakeyama Yoshinari}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|en|Emperor Kōnin|WD=Q958662|lt=Emperor Kōnin|1=Emperor Kōnin}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|en|Tachibana no Hayanari|WD=Q1062686|lt=Tachibana no Hayanari|1=Tachibana no Hayanari}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892534}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|WD=Q3273210|lt=goryō beliefs|1=Goryō}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:上御霊神社]] {{Commons category|Kamigoryō-jinja}} [[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]] {{wikidata link|Q11359018}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

{{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Sites of the Kami and Buddha Pilgrimage|1=Sacred Sites of Kamigata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10444949}} No. 100 (Kyoto No. 20)
{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city |location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|qid=Q1321824|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.036389|N|135.761667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō-ku|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|qid=Q3273210|lt=goryō spirits|1=Goryō}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|qid=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|qid=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|qid=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine|1=Chinjusha}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11489354}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsenen|en|Shinsenen|qid=Q11590468|lt=Shinsen-en|1=Shinsenen}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10892534}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|qid=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|en|Shitoku|qid=Q1146202|lt=Shitoku|1=Shitoku}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|en|Hatakeyama Masanaga|qid=Q5680891|lt=Hatakeyama Masanaga|1=Hatakeyama Masanaga}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|en|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|qid=Q11578676|lt=Hatakeyama Yoshinari|1=Hatakeyama Yoshinari}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|qid=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|qid=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|en|Emperor Kōnin|qid=Q958662|lt=Emperor Kōnin|1=Emperor Kōnin}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623103}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|en|Tachibana no Hayanari|qid=Q1062686|lt=Tachibana no Hayanari|1=Tachibana no Hayanari}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11499944}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10892534}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|qid=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|qid=Q3273210|lt=goryō beliefs|1=Goryō}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:上御霊神社]] {{Commons category|Kamigoryō-jinja}} [[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]] {{wikidata link|Q11359018}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

{{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = May 18 (Goryō Festival) |Map = Japan Kyoto city |location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|qid=Q1321824|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.036389|N|135.761667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{osm box|w|555300215}} '''Kamigoryō Shrine''' ({{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, Kamigoryō Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō-ku|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}. The shrine's official name as a religious institution is simply "Goryō Shrine" (御靈神社), and it is classified as a former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. The name Kamigoryō ("Upper Goryō") Shrine contrasts with {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}}, the "Lower Goryō Shrine." == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when epidemics spread across the land. These outbreaks were attributed to the curses of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|qid=Q3273210|lt=goryō spirits|1=Goryō}}, and in {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|qid=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 13 (794 CE), the vengeful spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|qid=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}} (posthumously deified as Sudō Tennō) was enshrined here. It is said that the shrine also served as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|qid=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine|1=Chinjusha}} for the Izumo clan's temple, {{ill|Kamizumo Temple|ja|上出雲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizumo Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which existed before the capital's relocation to Heian-kyō. In {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 5 (863 CE), a {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11489354}} ritual was held at the {{ill|Shinsenen|en|Shinsenen|qid=Q11590468|lt=Shinsen-en|1=Shinsenen}} to appease six spirits, including Sudō Tennō. This event marked the foundation of both Kamigoryō Shrine and Shimo-Goryō Shrine. Over the years, two additional deities, {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10892534}} and {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|qid=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}}, were added to the original six spirits. In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was known as "Kamizumoji Goryō-dō" (Upper Izumo Shrine) and enjoyed imperial patronage. By {{ill|Shitoku|en|Shitoku|qid=Q1146202|lt=Shitoku|1=Shitoku}} 1 (1384 CE), it was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}, the highest shrine Rank. In {{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (1467 CE), a conflict occurred at the shrine when {{ill|Hatakeyama Masanaga|en|Hatakeyama Masanaga|qid=Q5680891|lt=Hatakeyama Masanaga|1=Hatakeyama Masanaga}} clashed with {{ill|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|en|Hatakeyama Yoshinari|qid=Q11578676|lt=Hatakeyama Yoshinari|1=Hatakeyama Yoshinari}}, an event known as the {{ill|Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|御霊合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Kamigoryō Shrine|1=Battle of Goryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11489355}}. This skirmish was the prelude to the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|qid=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}}, earning the shrine the title "Birthplace of the Ōnin War." The shrine has also been associated with the {{ill|Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-oi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Izumo Takano Shrine|ja|出雲高野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though continuity is unclear. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, Kamigoryō Shrine was classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines eight primary deities, known collectively as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Sudō Tennō ({{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|qid=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|en|Emperor Kōnin|qid=Q958662|lt=Emperor Kōnin|1=Emperor Kōnin}}) * {{ill|Empress Dowager Inoue|ja|井上大皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Dowager Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (wife of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|他戸親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kōnin) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623103}} (mother of {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) * {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|en|Tachibana no Hayanari|qid=Q1062686|lt=Tachibana no Hayanari|1=Tachibana no Hayanari}} * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11499944}} * {{ill|Fire Thunder God|ja|火雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Thunder God|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 火雷大神|1=Yakusanoikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10892534}} (collective spirit of the six aforementioned deities) * {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|qid=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}} Five additional deities are enshrined in the auxiliary shrine: * {{ill|Ogura Family Deities|ja|小倉一族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura Family Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ogura Jitsuoki, Ogura Koretsune, and others) * Wako Myōjin ({{ill|Sugawara no Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|qid=Q3273210|lt=goryō beliefs|1=Goryō}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:上御霊神社]] {{Commons category|Kamigoryō-jinja}} [[fa:معبد کامی‌گوریو]] {{wikidata link|Q11359018}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

Kazuko|ja|菅原和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東坊城和子}}, associated with Sugawara family) == Cultural Significance == The shrine reflects the historical importance of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|qid=Q3273210|lt=goryō beliefs|1=Goryō}} in Heian Japan, commemorating those who suffered tragic deaths during political strife. It continues to serve as a religious and historical site, preserving the memory of Kyoto's turbulent history. == External Links == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/004/index.html Official Website (Kyoto Shrine Association)] * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/suehiro-mikosi/ |title=末廣神輿 上御霊神社 |date=20100217171901}} {{translated page|ja|上御霊神社|version=101246207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kamigoryō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11359018|co|commons:Category:Kamigoryō-jinja|fa|معبد کامی‌گوریو|ja|上御霊神社}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
